title: Add $aldff and $aldffe: flip-flops with async load.
body:
This is just a proposal of the cell interface, for discussion.  If accepted, the implementation plan is:

1. Add the cells to `kernel/rtlil.*` and other places having the list of cells.
2. Modify `FfData` to add fields representing async load. For consistency and to simplify some code, I'd also propose changing the latch representation to use the new async load / async data fields instead of the current approach of using the same data / enable fields as FFs. 
3. Add minor changes to passes handling `FfData` to handle the new functionality:
   - `kernel/ffmerge.cc`: simply recognize the new fields
   - `kernel/satgen.cc`: FFs with async set/reset are already not supported, extend the same treatment to async load
   - `memory_dff`: reject the new FF types for read port merging
   - `write_verilog`, `write_cxxrtl`, `zinit`, `clk2fflogic`, `async2sync`, `simplemap`, `wreduce`, `pmux2shift`: handle them the obvious way
   - `opt_expr`: add to the "merge inverters" list
4. Add support to `opt_dff` for optimizations involving the new cell type:
   - convert async load with const data to plain async reset
   - remove async load with always-off enable
   - replace async load with always-on enable with a buffer
   - replace a FF that has async load and const clock / always-off clock enable with a latch
   - (discuss this one) recognize dffsr with set/reset signals that emulate async load, convert to aldff
5. Add support to `dfflegalize`:
   - use `aldff` / `aldffe` to map "simpler" cells: `dff`, `dffe`, `adff`, `adffe`, `dlatch`
   - when not available, do the usual horrible async FF emulation dance with a warning
6. Add code to `proc_dff` to actually emit the cell when appropriate
7. Add FF mapping rules for ECP5+nexus (replacing current rules for dffsr and dlatch, letting `dfflegalize` handle them); may also consider sf2 and (non-ALM) intel, but both of those are pretty much unmaintained at the moment and I'd rather avoid messing something up here
